exemplar.title:
Saltburn and the all-consuming desire for power: How is the symbolism from Metamorphoses used to portray power and privilege in

Saltburn?

detailedExemplarData.title:
Saltburn and the all-consuming desire for power: How is the symbolism from Metamorphoses used to portray power and privilege in

Saltburn?

subject.title: English Lang & Lit (Old)
subject.description: IB English Language & Literature combines the study of language and literature, analyzing both literary and non-literary texts across various media and contexts. Students explore how language shapes meaning, identity, and culture through critical examination of diverse text types including advertisements, speeches, novels, and digital media. The course develops analytical skills, cultural understanding, and critical thinking about communication in different contexts and purposes.

strengths: Topic and purpose communicated clearly and consistently throughout the essay
Research question is explicitly stated and effectively connected to each section
Section headings maintain focus and prevent substantial digression
weaknesses: Minor lapses in phrasing that slightly obscure academic polish
Introduction and conclusion do not explicitly restate the research question
Limited justification of choice of nonâscholarly sources in methodology
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Strand assessments place communication and research question in the 5â6 band but note small imperfections and occasional drift toward summary, supporting a score of 5 rather than 6. Methodology is appropriate and relevant but lacks full scholarly justification, placing it at the top of the 3â4 band; averaged holistically, a score of 5 fairly reflects clear focus with minor methodological gaps.

strengths: Demonstrates solid knowledge of Ovidian myths and their filmic counterparts with accurate use of primary evidence
Generally accurate and consistent use of subject-specific terminology (e.g., mise-en-scÃ¨ne, Icarus complex)
weaknesses: Heavy reliance on popular media sources rather than peer-reviewed scholarship limits depth of understanding
Occasional typographical and citation errors weaken precision (e.g., misspellings, incomplete publication details)
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The work shows good, coherent understanding of both myths and film and mostly accurate use of terminology, meriting a 4 in the 3â4 band. Surface-level engagement with academic scholarship and minor lapses in precision prevent elevation to 5 or 6.

strengths: Research is clearly connected to the question through director interviews and visual analysis
Visuals (stills, captions) are well integrated to support claims
Creative intertextual readings (e.g., moth metaphor, horns as devil motif) demonstrate insight
Sustained scene analysis shows how symbols chart shifts in power dynamics
weaknesses: Overreliance on popular sources; more peer-reviewed criticism would strengthen relevance
Analysis sometimes slips into descriptive summary with few counter-arguments
Limited critical evaluation of broader cultural or alternate interpretations
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: All three strands of research relevance, analysis effectiveness, and argument evaluation fall solidly in the âgoodâ 4â6 band but lack the consistency and depth to reach 7â9 or above. A mid-band holistic score of 7 (out of 12) reflects strong but not exceptional critical thinking.

strengths: Meets all structural requirements (contents, headings, captions, consistent citation style)
Logical division into standard sections with clear navigation
weaknesses: Minor formatting inconsistencies (broken URLs, varied indentation)
Redundant or misaligned headings in the table of contents
Dense text in places would benefit from additional subheadings or paragraph breaks
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Structure fully satisfies the top band descriptors, but formatting errors and occasional layout lapses limit the score to 3 rather than a perfect 4. The essay remains easy to follow, yet could improve polish and consistency.

strandSelections.reasoning: The methodology (close scene analysis supported by mythological and interview sources) is broadly appropriate and the bibliography shows a range of primary and secondary material.  Nonetheless, several sources are journalistic or web-based rather than scholarly, and there is limited explanation of why these were selected over academic criticism of Ovid or film theory.  Choice and application are therefore âmostly completeâ rather than fully robust, aligning with the 3-4 band; the stronger end of that band (4) is justified by the quantity and basic relevance of material.

Formatting is generally correct: word count, title, numbered pages, in-text citations and a bibliography are supplied.  Minor inconsistencies (broken URLs, occasional line-wrap errors, varied indentation) show some lapses, so a score of 3 within the top band is appropriate.

The bibliography lists a dozen sources and citations are embedded throughout.  While some sources are popular media, each is clearly connected to the research question (e.g., director interviews contextualise symbolic intent).  Gaps in peer-reviewed scholarship prevent the research from being âconsistentlyâ relevant, yet it is more than simply adequate; hence mid-band (5).

The topic â the use of Ovidian symbolism in Emerald Fennellâs Saltburn â is stated in the title, reiterated in the opening paragraph and consistently re-signposted through section headings (âThe Wings of Icarusâ, âThe Minotaurâ, âThe Labyrinthâ).  Each section keeps the reader oriented around the guiding idea of power and privilege, and there is no substantial digression.  Minor lapses in phrasing (âorientating aroundâ, âprodogÃ©â) do not seriously obscure meaning.  Overall, communication is clear and purposeful, meriting the upper band, but the prose is not so polished or academically concise as to justify the full 6 marks.

There is a coherent through-line arguing that mythic symbols chart shifts in power, and the conclusion returns to the three motifs.  Yet evaluation of wider implications (e.g., cultural significance, alternative readings) is limited, and some assertions about âcoercive powerâ are accepted rather than critically tested.  These weaknesses place the discussion at the lower end of the âgoodâ band; score 4.

The essay offers sustained scene-by-scene analysis showing how imagery of wings, bulls and mazes signifies power.  Claims are usually supported by stills or script quotations.  However, the reasoning sometimes becomes descriptive (plot or costume summary) and counter-arguments are scarce, so the analysis is âgoodâ rather than âexcellentâ.  A middle value of 5 captures this solid but not outstanding depth.

The essay is divided into standard EE sections, includes a contents page, visuals are captioned, and citations follow a consistent style.  All elements of required structure are present, so it meets the descriptors for the top band in Presentation.

The student demonstrates solid knowledge of both the classical myths and the film text, accurately summarising Icarus, the Minotaur and the Labyrinth and linking them to specific cinematographic moments.  Use of still images and time stamps evidences familiarity with the primary film source.  However, engagement with classical scholarship remains surface-level and the essay relies on popular articles (GQ, Tumblr Q&A) more than academic criticism, preventing the work from reaching an âexcellentâ descriptor.

Film-studies terminology such as âmise-en-scÃ¨neâ, âwide-angle birdâs-eye viewâ and âover-the-shoulder shotâ is generally accurate and applied correctly to examples.  Mythological and psychological terms (âIcarus complexâ, âreferent powerâ, âcoercive powerâ) are defined and used with reasonable consistency.  Occasional spelling mistakes and mis-phrasing show inconsistency, so the work fits comfortably in the âmostly accurateâ 3-4 band; score 4 recognises the breadth of terms employed.

The research question â âHow is the symbolism from Metamorphoses used to portray power and privilege in Saltburn?â â is explicitly stated on the title page and functions throughout as the organising focus of the analysis.  Each analytical section ties back to that question by showing how a mythic symbol operates in the filmâs depiction of social hierarchy.  Although the question is tightly framed, it occasionally drifts toward general plot summary; therefore a secure but not perfect 5 is most fitting.

annotations.comment: Citation of Kamhi (n.d.) for the Ovid text is unclear; provide publication details or a stable URL to strengthen source credibility.
The title succinctly conveys the focus on Ovidian symbolism and power in Saltburn, setting a clear purpose from the outset.
The inclusion of a word count confirms adherence to the 4000-word limit, meeting the formatting requirements.
The table of contents demonstrates a clearly structured plan and ensures navigation through the sections is straightforward.
Several headings in the contents appear repetitive or misaligned (e.g., two âIntroductionâ entries); consider consolidating to avoid redundancy.
The myth summary is informative but leans heavily on narrative detail; link these elements more directly to your research question for sharper focus.
Effective use of the term âIcarus complexâ and link to social ascensionism demonstrates clear understanding of psychological concepts.
Bibliography shows a wide range of media but relies heavily on popular sources; integrating more peer-reviewed criticism would enhance research depth.
Several URLs in the bibliography are broken or line-wrapped incorrectly; review and correct to ensure all sources are accessible.
The introduction ends abruptly and does not explicitly restate the research question; complete the thesis statement for clearer framing.
Consider adding a brief rationale for selecting journalistic sources over peer-reviewed scholarship to justify methodological choices.
Typographical error: âdonned by Henry A. Murreyâ should be âcoined by Henry A. Murray.â Correct terminology to maintain precision.
Justify the choice of Sperberâs article over classical scholarship by briefly explaining its relevance to your methodology.
Figure 1 is well-integrated with analysis, and the caption clearly locates the scene in the film timeline.
Caption formatting inconsistency: film titles and italics are missing; standardize according to citation guidelines.
The link between Votawâs concept of referent power and the Icarus motif could be more explicit to strengthen your symbolic argument.
Insightful reading of the over-the-shoulder shotâs lighting to convey power dynamics.
The assertion linking light/dark contrasts to innocence vs. villainy would benefit from a film-theory source to underpin the claim.
The moth metaphor for Oliver is compelling and shows creative intertextual analysis.
Provide the script location or complete bibliographic reference for the p.111 citation to improve traceability.
Section header hierarchy is inconsistent (H2 followed by H4); ensure uniform styling for readability.
Clarify the distinction between behavioural and inner selves with direct definitions to avoid ambiguity.
Strong symbolic reading of the horns as a devil motif adds depth to your portrayal of Oliverâs inner nature.
Include full in-text citation details for âThe Myth of the Minotaur (2023)â to maintain scholarly rigor.
The extended analysis of the mazeâs dual routes is insightful and well-supported by Sandgrenâs commentary.
The dense exposition on the labyrinth journeys could be broken into shorter paragraphs or subheadings for improved clarity.
The conclusion effectively synthesizes the three motifs, but it does not restate the research question explicitly; include it to reinforce focus.
